To learn new ways of looking at the world and make sense if it, not just Iran and the middle east but in general. She asks questiobs that change your view and definition of many conventions. already signed up for my next class with her next semester. a proper Oxford-trained prof. meticulous, analytical, serious and humorous. I wish Penn had more instructors like her. I never had to force myself to go to this class and never felt sleepy while in class. and believe me, that's a HUGE plus for me! I can fall asleep easily. lol

I had fun in this class. The class is focused on a chronological study of Persian poetic tradition. A very good survey course to start from scratch if you don't know anything about the topic. classes are interactive and fun. planning to take her modern lit course next spring!
A fair professor. I learned some fun stuff. But I wouldn't take again. I guess there's not a healthy atmsphr in her classes. Sort of a weird tension. Maybe it's just me though.
Out of focus in so many occasions and ruined my GPA. Thank you so much!
Tough but respected. You can't get away with not doing your homework but it's worth it. Lots to explore in her classes. I recommended it to all my peers.
Amazing Prof. Very humble and knowledgeable and also accessible outside the classroom if you need help.

Took her course for extra credit and learned a lot about contemporary Persian lit. She puts the literary texts into their social contexts which is great. You end up knowing so much more than you expected about Iran.

I am about to graduate. My last semester at Penn. Truly the best professor I've met/learned from. I learned SO much from her classes. Things I would have never heard elsewhere about Iran and it's rich culture and literature. I will miss your classes Prof. Shams! :(

I am currently taking one of her courses. My best intro course to the Middle East so far. Not too difficult to struggle or panic. She is very understanding and extremely knowledgeable. Get ready to work hard, but it's totally worth it. Your writing and thinking skills will enhance massively just in a month time.

If you are looking for a different and innovative take on Iran, TAKE at least two of her courses: NELC 217 which is literature of modern Iran and NELC 218 which is Media and Culture in Contemp Iran. Both are amazing, intense and full of new materials for reading/learning. I am planning to take her NELC 216 in Spring.

Professor Shams! I took two courses with you, one in fall 2017 and one spring 2018. You have been a great source of inspiration in my life at Penn. I can't thank you enough for opening my mind and eyes about how to see/read/understand one of the most complex societies on the map called Iran. THANK YOU FOR EVERYTHING!

I learned a great deal in her classes. You need to read the material and think through them so it takes time but it's really worth it. She is a poet so her insight to reading the poems makes the experience of learning about Persian poetry with her pretty unique. I'm planning to take her other course in fall.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Inspirational

reviews.comment: Almost a fun class, but learned nothing. I believe she tried to be nice, but didn't have much to teach. Fine overall.

Took BIOE4010 with her as a freshman and it was one of my favorite class. For one, the topics covered were great at providing a foundation for the ways in which bioethical and philosophical (to a small degree)  arguments  are made. You don't really have to be 100% present during lecture and there is only one paper that is graded very fairly.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great lecture giver, she knows her stuff, she will explain really well. You are well prepared for tests. She is willing and ready to help in any way possible.
She is a great lecturer, has a great sense of humor, and is very willing to work with her students.
She helped me get jobs and further my career and was a great mentor overall.
Terrible.  Used the "R" word in class.  Incomprehensible. Offensive, and culturally insensitive.
She is an amazing teacher!! She helped me with all my troubles!!! If you guys take her course, you will find an excellent job!!

He's not a bad teacher, but is perhaps the worst in terms of organization and cannot make schedules for students. He is good educator, but terrible in terms of administration. There's no logic as to why we have remote 3-in-1 exam as this clearly does not prepare us in anyway in the future. The 1 exam/week was way better, stay clear of Penn Dental.

In my opinion,Dr. Alawi is the best professor we have had in the first two years of our dental school education. His lectures are heavy with material, but he really engages us and inserts humor to lighten the mood. His class notes are hilarious. I wish we could clone him and have him teach some of our other courses, especially oral medicine.
This man should be running the department and the course.  He is an amazing lecturer.  I think I speak for most of my classmates when I say that I wish he taught us more than he does.

Dr. Alawi is one of the best professors we have at Penn SDM.  He may be one of the most accessible also.  I just love him. His humor is not slapstick funny, but it lightens the mood of his classes.  I also love the personal anecdotes he incorporates into the lectures.  I know that some of the girls (like me!)have a bit of a crush on him.
He is by far one of the best professors we have at SDM. Easy on the eyes too. :) We need more people like him in our faculty.
I took "Gothic Bodies" with Professor Gamer in the Fall of 2025. Fascinating reading, and I've never had a professor who took such care with my writing. Best course I've taken at Penn.
Energetic and enthusiastic professor; knowledge of the material amazing. Showed me not just how to read Austen but also how to think about why we read, and what reading and interpretation are.
Good class, interesting though difficult assignments

cool (but difficult) assignments.  nice professor.   took his jane austen class my freshman year and even though it isn't even close to my major, it's still one of my fave classes so far.  really interesting discussions.
No Comments
No Comments
Gluck my goat fr, dont ever hate on him. Learned a lot, except i kinda didnt. TA's were also W.
A bit on the older side, you wont learn much in lectures but such a fun guy and the TAs are great in recs. Recommend 100%.
Amazing prof. Class is fun and the TAs are chill graders. Take 100% over 2400.
Amazing prof, really helps you truly understand the proofs behind mathematics

Class was interesting, inspiring, and very broad introduction to modern geometry-topology, with topics ranging from differential forms and tangent bundles to knot theory. Main problem was that the breadth of subject material and at times unreasonable amount of homework did not permit time to learn proofs in-depth. Maybe assign group work per topic.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's even worse than last semester. Doesn't prove anything that can't be done by just drawing a picture. By not proving anything he can keep a very fast pace, but keeping up with the textbook is impossible (unless you don't want to understand proofs i.e. you don't want to understand at all). Please go teach undergrads as they don't want rigor.

A problem with the course is assigning so much homework that there isn't enough time to read the text. This is a problem, because of the lack of rigor. For someone already familiar with the material, I would guess the course is pretty ideal. For everyone else the course is far from it. The 2nd year grads also seem to either love it or hate it.

My favorite course this semester! The class covers too much material to go through the proofs in class, so we spend most of the class time with examples; proofs are assigned as homework reading or exercises. (There are good proofs in the literature but few good examples!) Decent lecturer. Looking forward to 601!

Gives no definitions and very few rigorous proofs. Ignores the fact that a 1st year graduate course in differential geometry is supposed to teach tools and techniques with the goal of being able to use them in other fields. His course is equivalent to teaching grad cosmology out of Hawkins' A Brief History of Time. Useless and worst course ever.
When I had Professor Gluck, he took particular care to make sure that every student understood what we were learning. I thought he was excellent.
really nice guy, tries to keep class animated, but just not possible with the material

Professor Gadsden is a renowned scholar, but the course relied mostly on student-led discussions of dense readings, with little direct instruction. Feedback was brief and general, and I often left unsure what I learned. Discussions were very U.S.-centric and difficult for international students to engage.

Dr Gadsden is such a sweetheart. She organised a party at the end of class where she bought food for the entire class. She's so knowledgeable and goes out of her way to help every student. Students love her too. Was really lucky to be in her class and benefit from her intellectual generosity. Wish all teachers were as genuine and sweet as her.

Class discussion based which can be great & really informative but she doesn't chime in other than to say "you know thats an interesting point". She never makes a statement/opinion clear enough to feel like I actually understood her stance. She leaves everything in a gray area w/o an "answer". Syllabus changes weekly which is v frustrating.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Imperative course about multicultural issues in education.

If you get the syllabus, don't even bother with it because it'll be changed at the last minute. Group projects are the most annoying part of this course. The overall environment is not welcoming. There's just so many issues with this course. Avoid if you can. I have mixed feelings about the prof & her TAs. The class is very disorganized.

Vivian is great--a little intimidating at first but very warm once you get to know her, also very interested in helping her students. She does amazing work and she's happy to bring students into it, grad and doc alike. Take this class and change your perspective!
He's wonderful. I wish there were more courses that he teaches so I actually enjoy learning the content instead of getting through class for the grade.
He was so chill y'all

He is very clear with his guidelines. I always know what to do to get a good grade on his midterms and tests. His syllabus is WATERTIGHT and he somehow always brings the most fun and engaging energy to his lectures. He made his 3 hour lecture go by in a minute because that's how engaging he was.

This administration needs to purge this den of future DEI hires and communists known as University of Pennsylvania. I pray and hope Elon show no mercy to people like this. God saved Trump so that he can cleanse the world of lesser people that will corrupt this country and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN.

What does he want for a test answer, the world may never know. Get ready to write proofs with "assume not" turns of phrase. I got As in all my comp sci courses at Penn but this one. He came in the day after a test with his hair straight up and muttered to the class "you did not study". I even got book answers from homework that were marked wrong.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Class is hard, but he did a good job!

I took Professor Ghrist's Single Variable Calculus Part 1 Course on Coursera to help me prepare for Calculus I in college, and let me just say it absolutely did that job. His lectures were very detailed however his homework was a bit tough... Overall, I would recommend taking this course if you have enough knowledge from previous math levels.

Prof G is a very knowledgeable professor and knows a lot about math. However, the weekly quizzes were stressful and difficult, and he only briefly covered what was on the quizzes, choosing to show proofs of what we needed to know for quizzes. That would've been helpful if the quizzes covered that or if we were a proofs-based class....very bad class

This professor lied about providing support for missed class, and lowered my uncurved A to a C+ because I missed a final due to severe COVID. When I missed questions on the makeup final, he assumed I would miss those questions on the quizzes I didn't even take when I was sick and gave me complete zeros. Highly don't recommend taking his class.

Sir Robert Ghrist was the Single Variable Calculus Course on Coursera which I happened to take. I will proudly state that "His Lectures eased up CALCULUS for me." Best part of his course is that he explains all the applications of mathematics which is very rarely explored in other Maths courses. Also the quality of teaching is great.

Ghrist is AMAZING! His class was the hardest class I've ever taken, but it was also the most rewarding (even though the grade wasn't stellar). If you don't come to class or you don't pay attention, you won't get to realize the shear genius and awesome-ness that is a ghrist lecture. Plus, he's always willing to put time in to help you out.
passionate about subject and hilarious

He is a very entertaining lecturer and puts interesting twists on familiar mathematical concepts. I throughly enjoyed his class! *** He says that he teaches his classes in either "relax or kill mode." This class was laid back, but a few of his classes cover a brutal amount of material in a very short period of time.
Every question is a trick question on his tests. Applying and connecting concepts never explained in lecture. Terrible teacher. Terrible tests. Sassy person.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
BIOL 121 is def difficult, be prepared for lots of content and some difficult concepts.

But Goulian is one of the 2 or 3 best lecturers/seminar profs I had at Penn: coherent, every word accurate and precise (I'm someone who notices), knows what he's talking about. I learned a lot, didn't have to waste time figuring if he said something wrong etc.
For an introductory class, this was a hard one. But Goulian did his best at office hours to make the content digestible to all students. Without his help, I would have failed.

The deep learning class is good overall. The content is very relevant, especially for modern deep learning. The professor explains the codes in detail, which was great. I just wish the homeworks pushed us a bit more, but the final project looks tough.
Also he literally doesnt post anything to canvas (lecture notes, recitation notes, exam answers) unless you literally beg him on piazza.

Performative academia final boss. He literally just flexes meaningless formula derivations to us the entire time in lecture and then gives us 20 hours of completely unrelated homework. Exams are edge case probs with 40% average, and he pumps out papers like a robot so literally what is the point. 

Also its for ESE 5420 but that wasn't an option.

Fantastic professor! Explains complex AI concepts with clarity and enthusiasm, making even the theory-heavy parts engaging. Lectures are well-structured, along with real-world examples. Very approachable and responsive to questions, both in class and over email. I always walked away with a solid foundation and excitement.
The lectures successfully spiked my curiosity in taking a statistical approach to Data Science.

His course was one of the best deep learning courses I've taken at Penn. I learned exactly what I wanted out of the course. I loved the way he structured lectures as a colab notebook plus a few slides, and regularly go back to review the notebooks created for lectures for my own personal projects.
I liked the teaching. Exams were tough.
Great prof
No lecture recording. Meanningless Homework.
And you don't need to even ask anyone. Just check how many papers he and Pappas are writing every year and just see the content of the papers. It's like sooooo obvious
Lol i think the positive review below is written by him only. Like everyone knows he along with Pappas are writting papers like a factory

After each lecture I thought I had become an expert on the subject but this lasted until a homework was released. Even though I did not get the best grade I learned a lot. He is a good teacher and he genuinely cares about the students. I took his course on statistics&data science, but would definitely take any other course that he offers.
He is an amazing prof who works closely with his students. I have had courses with him and also done research under his supervision. He has continued to support me after my graduation.
He is a paper writing factory. Not sure whats the point of working with him at all

Hamed is nice and sharp. He did really good in his PhD in information theory and he understands math very well. Although currently he is publishing papers like a factory which IMO is quite unacademic. Nobody can publish that many papers in a year. I don't think he cares about what students learn so doing PhD with him is just to advance his career

Prof. Holleran was probably the most prepared and engaged prof I had at Penn period. I took CBE160 and CBE231 with him. 160 was one of the hardest classes I ever took, and he was brutally intense, but by 231 I had rounded in to form and his lectures added a ton of value. The lectures are a must to actually understand the material, it's worth it.
curves his class wierd. normalizes it so he curves students down based on the class average, promoting more competitiveness in the classroom unecessarily
cut and dry

reviews.comment: Holleran is dope
Unfair grader. Did not enjoy his class AT all even from an academic perspective.

Highly recommend this course to anyone interested in the Middle East. Dr. Lustick shows his passion and extensive knowledge through his lectures, which always make his lectures interesting. Although not required, having background knowledge on the Middle East is extremely helpful. Some weeks heavy reading is assigned. The exams are difficult.

Gem of a teacher...crazy intelligent.  Go to all of his classes, not just because you must in order to do well on his exams, but just for the sake of learning something interesting.  One day you will tell people you were a student of Lustick's.
Gem of a teacher.  Go to all of his classes simply for the sake of hearing him talk, you will certainly learn something, and one day you will tell people you were a student of Lustick's

Great lecturer. Classes are very interesting and time goes by quickly. I did like his presentations of Spanish avantgarde and Dali. But you need to read and specially you ned to work if you want a good grade. He does not give As away
He was a great lecturer and very knowledgable. My favorite Spanish professor thus far.

Very intelligent man, He is passionate about the subject.  Do not miss class, he is a stickler on attendance but that makes sense for an Ivy league school, He is very interesting, his accent is awesome, he is good a explaining material.  If you do not do well in this class, you are simply not ready to take it.  Its an upper level
i love him ....he makes everything thats not interesting so interesting. He tries to understand everyone and is a great lecturer
Fantastic Teacher
Booooring!about as clear as mud...
She really motivated me to learn more biol. and become the successful bio trwacher that I am now.

Prof. Waldron's course is challenging.She is slightly boring,but provides a lot of information,which I was not ready for as an upper freshman.I took this course in the Spring of 1987.I admit I did not work as hard as I should have.I didn't like all of the reading involved.Good professor,though!
Great teacher.  Do everything as she says and you'll be fine.

Al is both an amazing human being as well as one of the most brilliant teachers I have ever had the privilege to study under. His focus on a pedagogy of inclusion, his pure love for his fellow humans, especially, but not limited to his students, is only matched by his knowledge and love of poetry.
AL IS THE BEST!!! No one compares

Al, that's what he'll want you to call him, is amazing! Truly everything you could ever dream of in a college prof. He honestly cares so much for his students' education. (Don't be afraid to ask for help/extensions)
Changed my life. Opened up poetry to me. Opportunity to be a better poet and reader. Al rocks.
Al was an amazing 'lecturer' - learning comes about because we discover things - not because he tells us.
ModPo is just a great course. Accessible, democratic, valuable and engaging. Prof. Filreis teaching style is always evolving and unique in its enthusiasm

Brilliant, passionate, and a gifted teacher, Al Filreis is by far the best professor I have ever encountered, online or IRL. I've taken his Coursera Modern Poetry course twice, and have enrolled for the next session. It is such a joy to be part of this international group of learners.
Amazing course. Amazing experience. Amazing professor.
My first MOOC.  I was fascinated with the whole process.  I came out of curiosity and stayed for the party without missing one assigned even though the course is ungraded.
Taught me how to close read a text. Highly recommend

Al Filreis is amazingly charismatic, generous, and passionate about the material, the course, the MOOC pedagogy, and especially the people. He teaches with a disarming style that inspires his students to stay engaged, dig deeper, and continue learning and sharing great poems and poets. Easily one of the top professors I have ever had at any level.

The videos were mostly talking heads with no differences in the point of view among the participants. The pace was very uneven. I was extremely interested in the subject and have gone to other on line classes in order to get different perspectives and interpretations. Communications were generally unclear. My time could have been better spent.
Al is passionate, knowledgable, encouraging and hip. Any student who has a class with him is extremely fortunate.
Al is passionate, knowledgable, encouraging and hip.
Incredible Experience, Excellent professor and staff

PROF.AL-Amazing: personally involved with so many students & accessible so many hours a day! Teaching style is top-level. Can't say enough good about this man. His course was a life-changing experience. I'll always be grateful. But rating categories seem more course related than prof. related. Al is outstanding  across the board! I took ModPo 2x!
ModPo with Al Filreis--who would believe that love and humanity could be measured in a MOOC?
Attendance not mandatory, but I am still going to class.  Simply the best.
Al Filreis is an enthusiastic, open-minded, intelligent, and welcoming prof, who's passionate about his subject, about teaching, collaboration, and discussion. His is an amazing, eye-opening course.
great

Superb and enthusiastic teacher. He makes interesting material even more fascinating. Take any class from this professor. You'll learn a lot more than you expected. He'll become one of your favorite teachers now and one of your most remembered teachers later.
Most excellent Mooc

An amazing life-changing course, taught by a passionate, exceptional professor. I doubt very much there is another of these types of courses being taught with this kind of enthusiasm and dedication, which is a shame. These are hopefully the classrooms of the future; we need to clone Al Filreis to make them a reality.
modpo: greatest course ever taken... so far... until next year, when modpo's back!

this online class advances the absurd proposal that it is possible to teach a course on modern american poetry to a football stadium full of people in a pedagogically valid manner and then proceeds to accomplish just that. this course doesn't give you a fish, it teaches you how to fish.
Warm, fair, gentle, intelligent, helpful
Al is an inspirational teacher and I'm just one more of thousands who found ModPo enormously enriching and rewarding.  Thank you!
Al brought such an incredible amount of passion and interest to this course and really made the experience so enjoyable and emotional for me. He really is a wonderful teacher
This is it. Al is the best. Modpo is more than a course, it's an international poetry salon on a mission.

Al is one of a kind. I was amazed at the extent to which the course was an emotional experience and not merely an intellectual one. One  senses a personal relationship with Al and the TA's even if you are thousands of miles away on a different continent. I would   love to meet them.
Wonderful professor.  Spent inordinate amount of time teaching this course.  Learned more in this course than in any other humanities course I have ever taken.

I really enjoyed the video sessions by Prof Al Filreis. He had a unique style which made learning such fun and at the same time one think deeply over issues that were universal.He is an inspiring teacher with a natural flair for skilled classroom presentations.

I did not imagine that an online course in poetry could be so compelling and involving for students.  Al reached out through forums, videos, weekly call-in programs, etc to let every student be engaged in poetry.  His passion for modern poetry was contagious!

Al did what I would not have thought possible: created an online environment where he was able to convey his knowledge and his passion for poetry to a huge audience. He did it through enthusiasm and commitment. His level of engagement was incredible. Anywhere you went in the discussion forums, Al was there. He's STILL there; and so are we.

Professor Al Filreis is both passionate and well versed in poetry. Areas of study as subjective and often abstract as this one require just such a teacher. In the on-line course, I was impressed by his commitment to reaching out to the 30,000+ students. He constantly appeared on numerous threads numerous times. Al gave us a key to  all poetry.

Al is very involved in the day activities of the class.  He was involved daily in discussion board and our FB Modpo group discussions.  I found myself immersed in the material presented in the class and the lessons and insights gleaned from Modpo have carried over into my day to day life.

Al Filreis made the course of Modern and Contemporary American Poetry incredibly interesting. What is very valuable in the work of Professor Filreis is that he tries not to leave without the answer any question, what could seem impossible in MOOCs.

He helps one see poets and writers in a new light as if they had been partially hidden in chiaroscuro until then. He expounds his ideas in socratic fashion, so that one comes to understand what one otherwise may have missed about their worth. Most of all he helps one feel they can be part of what literature is all about.
Great course. Fabulous professor. Awesome TAs.

He provided a challenging and satisfying overview of modern and contemporary American poetry.  His use of a roundtable discussion rather than lecture format provided an high-interest learning environment.

I took the inaugural Coursera MOOC for UPenn's English 88 called Modern and Contemporary Poetry with students from all over the world.  Prof Filreis taught me how to read a poem and made the process relevant to my life to the point that I plan to be his student forever.  He is fantastic and demanding (and deserving) of one's best efforts.

Excellent class. Al Filreis is an excellent teacher who seems be able draw your interaction even though you are 3,000 miles away taking this online course. He has really added to my poetry experience. Take this online class (Modern&Cont Amer Poetry) through Coursera next Sept (2013).
His enthusiasm and interaction with the students MADE the class! What a wonderful, engaging, and terrific course this was on Modern and Contemporary Poetry!

Al Filreis is truly one of the finest teachers I have encountered at any level from kindergarten through graduate school: scholarly but down-to-earth, pedagogically expert, creative, witty, warm, congenial, helpful, generous of his time and spirit. He IS ModPo. I would truly take anything he teaches.
Fantastic. Best course on any subject I have ever taken, and that's a lot of courses.
Great professor and excellent class I took with him on Coursera. You will love any class with Al- he's friendly, engaging, and helpful.
Professor Al had been a great teacher throughout the course. Totally changed my level of understanding the poetry.
He was the best Professor I have ever had.  Informative, educational, funny, and the learning from him changed my life.
Great learning experience with amazing format on Coursera. Al was the perfect facilitator!
Great Professor, great experience to take his online class!

Al Filreis is an amazing teacher. He gives me hope that there are still inspirational teachers out of poetry and English left in this world. Al's Modern & Contemporary Poetry class is top-notch. One of the BEST college classes I've taken and I'm 40 years old!

I would highly recommend any course this instructor thought was worth putting his time towards. He has a teaching style that makes challenging material absorbable. I learned so much during this 10 week course. Al is extremely organized, is accessible as much is humanly possible, and is passionate about the subject.
Highly recommend. I would be interested in taking any course of study he thought worth teaching.
Excellent teacher and person!!
I learned more about contemporary American poetry than I already knew about it before taking the course.
Prof. Al is the best teacher I've had.
MODPO: Great class, great Professor, great TAs! Highly recommended!
Wonderful person. Absolutely wonderful class - worth every moment of time I spent on the class - If only all Professors and Teachers were like this - the world would be better place
ModPo MOOC online course. Amazingly good!
Best professor ever-truly loves his subject and a very enthusiastic teacher"
One of the most interesting and coherent classes ever on modern American poetry, tied together through 10 weeks. Al Filreis is a model of how education should be reformed and available to everyone.
Coursera is amazing!
He's incredible
Al has set a new standard for future teachers that will be hard to meet. His knowledge and enthusiasm for poetry is contagious. i have found a new passion!

Al Filreis is an outstanding professor and knows how to be intimate with a student body of 35,000. He was appropriately challenging. Very exciting class and concept for an online class. I have lots of experience being taught and teaching modern poetry and he is the best!
Al has spoiled me for all future Coursera classes!

This was the MOOC course, Modern & Contemporary Poetry. This was the best course I have ever taken. Prof. Filreis' dedication and enthusiasm and his extensive knowledge provided an extremely positive experience. Given the size of the class, his ability to give every one of us a personal response from time to time was amazing.
The professor spent an extraordinary amount of his own free time engaging in discussion with the class members.
Superb presentations.  Never makes a student feel stupid for any opinion, even when he disagrees.  Very open and willing to engage with students.  Willing to spend the time needed to interact.

the numbers in my previous ratings are screwed up. I am trying to highlight an outstanding educational experience.This is the first use of this rating system for a cousera course. Perhaps it is problematic.
Al was a truly excellent professor.  He's the best teacher at Penn.
the Kindest person ever!!!!!!!
Alllllllllllll.......

Would take another class from him for sure. Classes were actually interesting and he implemented fun activities to help integrate the concepts discussed in class. Clear instructions which I appreciated.
Excellent Professor. Would highly recommend. For a required class, the discussions were stimulating and his offered great feedback during meetings and on papers.

He is so lenient and nice but he is frustrating. In the instructions it says write 850 words, then in class he says something else; Canvas page is so confusing; feedback sometimes is so useless. It's just little things that make the course such a frustrating experience.
"You are only responsible for every word I say in class" and we were, which means there is no need to read the textbook and every exam was as fair as can be.

Prof. Winker seemed like a decent guy, but he certainly relied on the textbook to teach. This is fine for those who have time, but it was really bad for students who were taking the course with other heavy science reqs to graduate. Once you fall behind, he's very blunt and recommends dropping out rather than turning around.

Prof Winkler is so entertaining, and I easily stay engaged during lectures (which is NOT the case for other orgo profs). He covers everything in lecture, and even offers retakes on the tests (i.e. two chances to do well). He has office hours 4+ times per week. He's a bit intimidating, especially if you talk to him one on one, but overall great guy.
Winks is the best. Hilarious and savage. Maybe the best prof ive ever had. He clearly loves chemistry and loves teaching. Hes the man!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Not a very good communicator, and taught esoterically. The exams did not reflect anything given in either the lecture or the reading material. Very abrupt and short when asked for help during office hours. Not empathetic when you came with questions that he felt you "should" have known the answers. One of the worst professors during my 4y at Penn.

He's terrible. Instead of helping me when I needed him most he encouraged me to drop out and forget medical school. Luckily I ignored him and am applying to match in vascular surgery this spring. If you're doubting yourself because of things he said to you- know it's not you- it's him.

Winkler can teach well enough, however on a personal level, he is demeaning, condescending, and makes efforts to shame students rather than help us. I love chemistry but he turns me away from it. He makes going to class or meeting with him a very unpleasant experience.
The man
went much much too fast. didn't care that nobody understood him.  made "jokes" that were demeaning.  just not a nice guy in the classroom.  unpleasant to be around.

Never answered student questions in lecture. He always referred us to videos made before class. Weekly quizzes were very difficult.

I heard that he was the math professor that you wanted. No matter what, get him. I will say that he explained concepts with enthusiasm and was eager about the content. But I did feel like he was a bit condescending, and even though I never missed a class or video, whenever someone would ask for clarification, he'd point to a video and not help

He is a rude instructor that rushes class and students questions. Always makes students feel dumb for asking questions, but then later apologizes when he notices there was a error on the part of the math department that caused them. Bad at communication, disorganized, and always late.
Teaches the most popular math courses on campus and is widely respected for his quality and energy he brings to all his lectures
The kid! Great, thorough teacher.

Prof Rimmer is a fixture within the Penn Math Department. I like him a lot, and had two classes with him, back to back. However, be forewarned - he is hard, and despite his easy-going lecture style, he is tough on certain mathematical pet peeves. Would only recommend taking him in an online course if very adequately prepared.

Literally am so sad that I won't be having him for all my math courses at Penn. He's a professor who actually likes to teach and wants his students to succeed. He's also willing to take any extra initiatives to make sure students understand the material.
Rimmer was an awesome professor! Honestly, if you don't do well in this class it's because you weren't really prepared to take it.
One of the best professors I've had at Penn! He really cares about his students and wants to see them succeed.

Great professor. Definitely takes time and effort in his work. He has so many resources that he lets students use so they can do well. He will always answer any questions you have. Tests are tough but lectures are always funny and entertaining. I would definitely recommend taking his class. Best math professor at Penn!
He's very dedicated to his teaching career.
No Comments
He is GREAT! His teaching actually makes you enjoy math. I want to continue taking courses with him.
I think he's great!!

I liked his teaching style a lot, especially since the material and text was so hard to understand. Unfortunately, he wasn't the person making the exams the year I had him, and the exams were common. So things got competitive. Then when it was time to do grade break downs a lot of my class had the same grade so every point mattered to the decimal.
He's a pretty cool guy. Definitely knows his stuff. He makes the occasional math error in lecture, but he's good about correcting himself.
Great Professor. Probably one of the best I've head at Penn. His lectures are cleary and he is extremely helpful. One of the few professors at this school that actually care about teaching.
Professor Prashant Purohit is very nice responsive and supportive.
No Comments
Good, clear, useful class
She is a fair grader, while also providing useful feedback on assignments. Pam is awesome and worth taking again!

Dr. Szczygiel is amazing! She is genuinely passionate about the work that she does. She is supportive and understanding. Taking her class on self-care is helpful and exciting. MSW students can easily get burnt out, but this class helps. There are group assignments which is fine. Reflection journals are weekly.

She is one of my favorite professors at this school. She makes the material interesting and engaging and I always looked forwards to her classes. She is very accommodating and understanding, and her assignments are not difficult.

Many Urdu speaking students take her class. If they are there to question, work, and improve, they do well. If they are there for the easy grade and don't take it or her seriously, this class will not suit them.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Really mean to students she doesn't like.  Kicked me out of the classroom one day for not bringing my textbook, but really, she was just trying to find an excuse because she didn't want me in the class anymore.  I wish I would have said something back, but I decided to just be polite.
No Comments

This administration needs to purge this den of future DEI hires and communists known as University of Pennsylvania. I pray and hope Elon show no mercy to people like this. God saved Trump so that he can cleanse the world of lesser people that will corrupt this country and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN.
He helped me with some lab related questions.
He's a cool guy, but I don't think he explains things well enough in class and goes quite fast, although he does do a short review at the beginning of every class. Homeworks in this class are a pain.
No Comments
No Comments

You can tell she loves to teach! She shares real life stories about her practice. She's compassionate and doesn't think her class is the most important thing you have to do. She's real and cares about students wellbeing. She can put you on the spot by asking questions in class. I enjoyed both the psychopathology and group class she taught.

Marsha is the best professor I have ever had (and I have 4 degrees from 3 universities). SHE IS A REAL HUMAN BEING--Not the typical Ivy League arrogant instructor. She will tell you exactly what she expects, and she will be there for you whenever you need her (her office door is always open). I can't say enough great things about her.

Professor did not show up to 2 of 8 evening classes and gave no notice of her absence leading to students going to campus and paying for parking only to not have class.  Professor used Canvas for all assignments and kept changing requirements.  She did not grade more than half of the assignments completed and gave random final grades to all.
Dr. Davies is an incredible instructor he is the most engaging professor I have ever had the pleasure of taking a course with.
One of the best professors I have ever had, really cares about each student and wants everyone to succeed.

Davies was an amazing professor! his interactive powerpoints made class exiting and engaging. I liked his jokes each class and I could tell that he put a lot of thought and effort into the structure of his class each week. He was also very helpful during office hours and clarified any confusion that I had.

Dr. Davies is an incredible professor. He communicates the subject material in an instructive and entertaining manner and is committed to making sure every student is able to succeed in his class. He excelled in transferring the course to a virtual format.
Dr. Davies puts in so much time to make things interesting and engaging to students and makes class fun (even though it had to be on zoom), amazing instructor who genuinely cares for students.

Prof Davies is one of the most entertaining chem teachers I've had. He is passionate about the content and it spreads to the students. He also loves to interact with the students and makes jokes that are always funny. I wish I could have him for all my chem courses.
Dr. Davies teaches in a very clear way. He gets straight to the point, and explains the material in a way that makes practical sense. I would definitely take more classes taught my Dr. Davies.

Best professor I have had yet. Very understanding of the struggles of school, but also encourages people to try in his class. Cares so much about his students and pushes us to learn. I loved this class so much, can't believe I'll miss Thermo!
Took intro to materials with Professor Davies; course was awesome, entertaining and interesting. Highly recommended to anyone wanting to learn more about MSE. Great professor!
A great professor. I truly enjoyed my time and learned a lot of things!
Professor Davies is an amazing professor who truly wants students to learn; best teacher I've had in my major, if not at this entire university.
The best professor I've had at Penn so far--and one of the best teacher's I've ever had.
Davies is engaging, funny, organized and entertaining uses excellent examples to engage his students; one of the best teachers on campus.  Take his course if you get the chance.
I took this course last semester; I have never had a better teacher . Dr. Davies cares so much about his students: awesome
Took thermo this spring, Dr. Davies spent many extra hours helping me understand concepts.  Really cares about students, best professor I've had so far.
Really good notes and explanations; actually makes Thermodynamics understandable.  Entertaining professor who cares about students and lectures - best I've had this year.
Chemistry is not my favorite subject, but this professor is. He  makes things clear and connects to interesting examples in technology.  Dr. Davies is by far my best prof.
All these comments are true, great professor and funny.  Kept my interest in a course that was hard for me.
Amazing lecturer - relates basic chem to emerging technology, great at keeping our interest.
Unbelievable at making incomprehensible material understandable and clear.  Best prof in the school, funny too.
Great jokes, very funny.  Connects basic chem to real applications, love this professor
Sooo helpful in office hours and clear in lectures.  Of all my engineering profs he is the absolute best
Currently taking frosh chem; all comments are true.  Fantastic prof, really funny but jokes have a purpose - they help you learn.  Best of all my current teachers
These ratings are true - clarity to the max and very funny.
Amazing at explaining difficult concepts - thermo rocks when Davies is teaching it.
Funny very clear explanations - you've got to take one of his classes
Amazing engineering prof; connects what can be boring material to interesting applications.  Very funny - go to his lectures and sit at the front.
Just outstanding
Awesome prof!
Very funny - made class enjoyable, very clear explanations.  Great accent!!!
best professor iv'e ever had at penn
Super mean
Would not recommend any course with this professor at any level.
Unfathomable amounts of reading, extremely long essays, terrible TAs, confusing lectures. Would never take a class with him again.

Possibly one of the most dynamic, intelligent, interesting and provocative professors at Penn. You get from Vitalis what you give - give a lot, you'll get nothing less than gold. Highly recommended no matter what your major - take his class. Do the readings. Argue. I'm just sorry I didn't work harder in the class, he is worth it.
Vitalis is helpful,  but his classes are by no means easy to get A's in.
i never wore my seatbelt driving to campus because i was always hoping i would not make it to this class
She was ok, but a lot of her outside of work opinions leaked into class and it was embarrassing at times. She demonstrated very poor judgement and it made me lose trust in her.

One of the best professors Ive ever had. Very nice, but politely applies pressure when necessary. She is an expert in stylistics, grammar and effective writing. (Always consider your audience!) She is very open-minded and willing to learn from her students. Class was nearly always full of productive discussions. Absolutely take her course!
Excellent teacher on so many levels.  Clear and organized thinking  while deeply insightful about the content.  Got a lot from taking her. Fave teacher at Penn hands down. (senior now)
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Been going through and rating some of my favorite professors so someone hopefully benefits and enrolls in one of their classes.  Val is awesome.  Just take anything she teaches.  usually it's "study of an auteur" about a filmmaker...forgot the course number
He seems nice but is ruthless, very scary. He hosted (NOT ALLOWED) online classes when the university declared a holiday

I was excited for this course, but I dislike the teaching style. The professor sometimes purposely solves problems incorrectly then corrects them, which confuses beginners. You are expected to expand upon concepts for HW and tests way beyond what is covered in lecture. Feels like I'm paying to have Google teach the material.

Doctor Val Tannen may know a lot in math (i mean, he got PhD). But he REALLY doesn't know how to teach. And I further doubt of his interest of teaching people who are not passionate about math.
For basic concepts, he gave the hard cold mathematical explanation, didn't bother to boil it down. Youtuber can explain the same concept better.

Without a doubt, my favorite class at MCIT. It changed the way I look at math, at computer science, and how I think. It was hard, for sure (only got a B but I am darn proud of that B), but 100% worth it. Lectures are great and thorough and the professor is strict but fair. Exactly the kind of professor I like, because he pushes you to grow.

I'm convinced this professor just doesn't know how to teach discrete mathematics, so he tells students to learn it themselves under the guise that thats the better way for them to be learning. this is an Ivy League education? I'd probably have a better experience at a community college.
Amazing prof, so hilarious and makes lectures enjoyable. super hard class but if you put in the work you will do well.

Lectures are set up awfully, with little instruction leaving the student to attempt to fill in the gaps. Blows my mind after seemingly years of students complaining that the course is unorganized and the lectures are just thrown together randomly they won't redo the class.

I've had a bad experience with the head TA in 592. While I understand the pressures of the TA role, I found her approach to student interaction to be often grumpy. Her responses to questions in class or on discussion posts frequently come in the form of rhetorical questions, which can be aggressive. It's surprising she continues to be the head TA..
Great professor. Tough but fair. Cares about his students. Not super accessible but does his job.

Lectures are all over the place and aren't sufficient themselves to get a good grade in the course (need to consult outside resources). Homework is difficult and requires you to attend TA hours to perform well. Anyone who says the professor is good and anyone complaining just isn't working hard enough clearly has never had a good prof before
grading requirements are not covered in the course not listed in the question. Good luck.

Horrible professor who doesn't check his emails after the first week of classes. 
His class will take up most of your time when studying and he expects students to know all the material before coming to class and expecting you to know how to solve complicated proofs and other problems. 
I highly recommend taking CIS1600 in the fall.

Many other reviews here are too harsh and likely written by disgruntled students. His class is very difficult for sure (I even had a nightmare once about it), but he is an extremely committed professor who is very fair in his decisions. He is passionate about the material as well. Make sure to attend all OOH and review sessions.

Discussion on homework is strictly prohibited, and TAs are not allowed to help with homework in any matter. Many times we need clarifications for very poorly worded questions but cannot be helped. Grading rubic is never clear, you get 0/10 using an alternate solution and write essays to seek regrade. High school maths deliberately made complicated.
His explanation is not clear at all, I am having a difficult time understanding his discrete math course
Wonderful professor, I think he is very fun and does in good job in teaching very difficult class.
Most of the assignments seem confusing

You must answer questions following the template the professor provided otherwise you will lose some points. For example, you will be punished for failing to mention some keywords even you get everything correct.
Awful

Professor Tannen does not get nearly enough credit. Many students who take this course lack strong Mathematical foundations, but that is in no way his fault. His lectures are clear. His office hours are extensive. He often goes overtime to ensure that no student questions are left unanswered. Reading comprehension is a must at a Master's level.

Terrible lectures do not set you up for success in homeworks or exams. Expect to learn mostly from outside sources and expect to only be able to do the homework after grilling the TAs for hints and pointers on how to approach the problems.
Professor Tannen is mean-spirited and tetchy.
Professor Tannen is abysmal and his grading is inconsistent and unfair. He's also mean spirited and tetchy.
Class is not set up well, the lectures are old and error-riddled and the professor does not seem open to changing the non-conducive learning style of this course.

While he is a smart man, that does not mean he should be a professor. 

His way with words could be considered lacking as his comments come off rude or sometimes cruel for pretty simple questions.  Very stuck in his ways.

For this course in MCIT he does not tends to realize he is teaching CS students and not people pursuing a Math Masters.

Such an awful teacher, he teaches a math course but does not actually teach you. He expects you to pick everything up yourself, with no clear elaboration on the class material. He chooses to make things harder than they should be (especially in terms of language), so you are tested more on question comprehension rather than discrete math itself.

The topics he explains and the ones he asks you to solve are no where related. I guess he is expecting us to learn math ourselves and not actually teach. The assignments are poorly worded you end up scratching your head to make sense of simple English than actually solving problems. Senseless questions poor grammar. You will end up as content creat

Great professor. Available to help students - always kept his open office hours going until there were no more questions, even if the scheduled time had elapsed. Felt challenged but never hopeless about the class, and really felt I had earned my grade at the end. I wish he taught more MCIT classes!
Great professor. Engaged with the students and clearly wants to help them to thoroughly understand the material. All the material has been very relevant to our follow on core classes.
Cold conversation. Doesn't care about the students' experience.
Great prof.
Does not explain concepts

Great professor. He did a good job of making the course easy. The assigned text was also easy to understand.
He will make you contemplate suicide.
hard class, moves fast.  he will help you (if you nag him)
Transport is by far, the most work I've EVER had...cut us some slack! He tries to be helpful if you ask though.
transport graduate style, enough said
the best !!!!
Seems like a nice guy, but does not explain things very well. Hard class overall
Almost impossible to understand. Also, not a very pleasant human being. Econ can be interesting, but he makes it dry, boring, and soul-crushing.
he's accent is hard to follow. But the lecture is quite interesting. Book is somehow useless here if you attend every lecture.
I love his accent, even though it's sometimes difficult to understand. He made the class very inetersting.

If you have never studied economics before than Spiegel will not make your life any easier. His lectures are very dry and he is very hard to understand due to his accent. He is an awesome economist but he fails to present his material in a coherent way. Spiegel loves math and equations so if you want economics with math than choose him.

His lectures are very difficult to understand unless you review the material ahead of time.  He does not present the material in an organized and logical way.  Lecture is not the best place to see the material for the first time.

Obviously the first kid's comment is idiotic, considering he ****ing misspelled thick (he put think). His accent isn't too bad, it's way too built up. He is math based, and gives you lots of ways to get the answer quickly if you attend lecture.
His accent was way too think. If it weren't for that, I might have actually been able to stay awake in class and learned something.
Tries to be helpful and is knowledgable, but has difficulty with technology and is hard to understand

Dr. Spiegel is a super nice man but his accent is very strong.  His class is more math based than other Econ 1 classes.  His lectures can be very boring, but he at least trys.  He is a readily avail in office hours, where he can help you a ton.
No Comments
Wants to be as helpful as he can, but you simply can't understand him in lecture.  He has a heavy accent and is technologically incompetant (either the mic doesn't work or it hisses constantly).

Dr. Urban is a wonderful professor. His lectures are interesting and very comprehensible. The tests are directly from lecture material (which can be found in online power points) as well as the reading material. The articles can be long and challenging, but overall Dr. Urban is excellent! I highly recommend his class!

This is the professor you wished you'd always had.  He often quotes Pokemon cartoons and wears entertaining clothes.  While the readings are only tangentially related to his lectures, his charisma made this class my favorite.
No Comments
Fun and interesting professor who always manages to engage his students to perform at the limits of their capabilities. One of the department gems.
great teacher - take his class even if you're a non-major
Enthusiastic without being annoying.  Interested in teaching, very well organized, wonderful anecdotes along the way.  Tests are fair and workload isn't impossible.

Her approach feels punitive and enforcement driven, more like dealing with a police officer than a social worker. The emphasis is on rigid rule compliance rather than support, understanding, or problem solving.

Trotta is not the supportive, student-centered professor people describe. It often feels like she does not meet students where they are, and the expectations can seem unclear and discouraging. Instead of feeling supported, many students feel stressed and overwhelmed. I would carefully reconsider before taking her.
If you challenge your grade, she will focus on the little mistake you made and offer no grace. She is out to get students!

Only take if no other professor is available. Otherwise, your goal of getting a 4.0 will be at risk over this professor's nitpicking and attempt to make the class much more difficult than it actually is at this level

I usually earn good grades through effort and participation. In this class, grading felt focused more on small writing details than on overall understanding and effort. The expectations for writing were stricter than I anticipated, and this affected my final grade. I would have benefited from clearer feedback and more emphasis on learning progress.
Makes her extremely easy class inaccessible
Has unrealistic writing requirements
Condescending

This professor is extremely subjective in her grading, lectures, and material. Not worth the headache.
Do whatever you can do avoid Trotta
The absolute worst class I have ever taken…
This professor is not fit to teach effectively. I would strongly advise against taking her class.

Have the goal of getting all A's? Don't take Trotta …
So bad I withdrew
Completely unprepared every day and repeats the same material
Among the BOTTOM 5 percent of professors world wide .  I can't believe they still allow her to teach .
I'm actually shocked she has any  positive reviews . Literally everyone in my class hated her
Class feels like walking barefoot on hot coals. Everything is unclear, guidance absent, grades arbitrary, lectures chaotic, spirit broken, brain fried, flee now.
I wouldn't wish taking her class on my worst enemy. So traumatizing….
If you want to actually gain skills, confidence, or a supportive learning environment, look elsewhere—this course is a trap.
Do not take this professor if you care about learning or mental health. The course is disorganized, grading is unpredictable, and questions are often met with condescension.

This course violated core social work principles. Instead of modeling ethics, empathy, and empowerment, it normalized intimidation, gatekeeping, and emotional invalidation. Students learned more about power misuse and harm than about trauma informed, ethical social work practice.

This course was chaotic, hostile, and profoundly unprofessional. Expectations changed constantly, grading felt retaliatory, and questions were treated as personal attacks. The professor modeled arrogance and control instead of social work ethics, creating an environment that felt unsafe, demoralizing, and deeply inappropriate for this field
she has an underlying expectation that you must know everything as if we are not students .

the glowing reviews are completely misleading. Her lectures are confusing, disorganized, and nearly impossible to follow. Students are left floundering to understand even the simplest concepts, and the class feels like a punishing exercise rather than a learning experience. It's easily the most frustrating and poorly taught course I've ever endured

Her instruction is very challenging, and many students find it difficult to follow her lectures. Like other professors at Penn, she presents concepts  in a confusing way, making it hard to fully understand the material. Additional focus on teaching methods could improve clarity and help students grasp the content more effectively.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: If you're not a strong writer, steer clear.  This class heavily weights writing ability, so getting an A is unlikely even if you grasp the material well.
She heavily emphasizes psychoanalytic theory, so this class may not be ideal if you prefer modern approaches or want a broader overview of interventions.

Professors like her are why people say Penns MSW doesn't live up to the hype .  Nothing about her is extraordinary, but she will act as if everything that comes out of her mouth is golden. It's outdated and not evidenced based on. She's very woo-woo…
Literally take anyone but her!  You will learn so much more and have much less stress…

She's so bad at teaching  that a group of students had a private meeting about her and even floated getting the department chair involved . The consensus is that she isn't good at teaching and belongs at a community college , not PENN.  We also agreed to all write reviews so that other students would have enough information to avoid her
She will make you hate social work
I have 3 degrees and she is by far the worst professor I have ever had

Ever have a professor who pressed trivial issues to the point that it became annoying and even detrimental to your performance in class ?  Those are the worst, right ? That's definitely this professor. Avoid at all cost!

Want to ruin your GPA and your confidence at the same time? Sign up. I was a stellar student before her class but she ruined me . I'm now applying to law school  to advocate against the many women like her who get into social work for power and control .

Her class is a nightmare from start to finish. Lectures wander aimlessly, assignments are meaningless busywork, and she offers no useful guidance. She acts like an expert but leaves students clueless and underprepared, turning what should be professional training into a frustrating waste of time and energy. Absolutely avoid if you want to learn.

Sarah's classes are disorganized and lack depth. She often digresses, making it hard to follow key concepts, and her explanations leave more questions than answers. Feedback is minimal or unhelpful, and she seems more focused on appearing knowledgeable than actually supporting student growth. Time in her class feels wasted, not educational.

Sarah is a truly exceptional professor. She's brilliant, funny and brings the material to life in a way that will stay with me for years to come. As a respected psychotherapist, her expertise is invaluable for clinical students. She's also incredibly generous with her time and genuinely cares about preparing the next generation of social workers.

The issue is not rigorous writing expectations but the overwhelming weight placed on them. Feedback rarely reflects actual knowledge or effort. The course lacks clear rubrics, creating a grading system that feels opaque, inconsistent, and vulnerable to bias. Formal criteria are urgently needed for fairness.
This “professor” had soo many more negative reviews on here . classmates said that when they wrote negative reviews , the  reviews were reported and removed . Same thing happened to me.

Her grading process was frustrating. Despite significant effort, she  was fixated on minor details of writing style, and the lack of any official rubrics made my final grade feel like a complete personal judgment call. It was clear that my performance was judged unfairly, resulting in a grade that did not reflect my understanding of the material.

She presents herself as an expert, but her cold, elitist demeanor and obsession with abstract psychological theory make her utterly incapable of providing anything of real-world value.  Any student  depending on her credibility would be setting themselves up for failure. You will “ experience” her as being antithetical to the profession.

Her arbitrary and capricious grading seriously harms students,especially non native English speakers or those with learning differences.Her approach wrongly treats writing ability as proof of social work skill, distorting competency and  significantly stifling real learning because students focus on avoiding penalties instead of mastering the work.

I did not find this class helpful at all. The teaching felt disorganized and the discussions did not support real learning for me. Feedback often felt closed off rather than welcomed, and the tone in class made it hard to engage. I left the course feeling frustrated instead of supported
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Sarah's classes are by far the best ones I have taken at SP2. She is brilliant, thoughtful, funny, and shares a wealth of invaluable clinical knowledge. She is open to feedback and her highest priority is that her students are learning and engaging in reflective practice. All you need to do is to show up and put in some effort. HIGHLY recommend!!

Doesn't take a rocket scientist to tell when someone writes their own reviews… Don't take this class if you want your actual thoughts and ideas critiqued. Even perfectly fine writing gets ripped to shreds, and the feedback focuses more on how to write like she wants rather than engaging with your work.
Amazing professor and remarkable human being. Absolutely made me a more a critical thinker and contributed significantly to any success I achieve as a social worker. Take her class.

One tiny mistake can turn a perfectly written paper into something “unacceptable” and brand you a poor writer.She slashes points for the smallest writing issues until your whole grade collapses. She grades as if we're all English majors instead of social work students and demands flawless,doctoral-level writing instead of master's level work.Awful.

Best professor at Penn. Trotta's ability to impart clinical theory, wisdom, and skill to her students is of an unparalleled intellectual caliber. She puts 110% into every class and expects her students to do the same. She is a gem of a human, an outstanding professor, and an amazing clinician. Students all want her to be our therapist.

Doc Trots is literally the best prof at SP2 but she makes you work hard. She's a perfectionist herself so she expects us to turn in good papers and think hard about becoming therapists. Only take her if you care about learning and want to be the best therapist you can be. Lectures are both funny and highly intellectual. She teaches complexity well.

Sarah goes above & beyond. Regardless of you being clinical or macro, I highly recommend taking her courses. She is brilliant & speaks using personal experience that help students relate to the material. She is by far one of the best professors at Penn in regards to not only her lessons but also her ability to know each student in a meaningful way.

Sarah is one of the most caring professors at SP2. She takes time out of her day to meet with students to talk about anything from class, to how field is going, or whatever you need to talk about. She takes the time to get to know each student & gives detailed & thoughtful feedback on assignments. By far one of the best professors I have ever had.

Sarah Trotta is a great professor who will teach you applicable skills for the field and new ways to analyze mental health. She is very accessible outside of class and will happily discuss your field work with you. She cares deeply about social work, her clients, and her students. I wish I had the opportunity to take another class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Sarah really aligns with her students and listens to us. Lectures are fascinating. She teaches us what we need to know and more.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Don't take Trotta if you don't have to.

Best professor i've taken at this institution; i have learned so much from sarah and she really goes out of her way to make sure that the material is properly explained so don't be afraid to ask questions! she is also very caring about the overall well being of her students. with that being said she is a stickler for attendance and tardiness.
Dr. Trotta has a way with words. She makes complex concepts simple and digestable.

One of the most engaging professors I have ever had the pleasure to learn from. Professor Venkatesh makes the subject come alive and knows the subject so well that he is able to give multiple triangulating explanations that paint a holistic picture of the underlying theories.
He is simply the best professor I have ever had. An interesting, rigorous introduction to probability from both a mathematical perspective and a historical perspective.  - Penn Student

Lectures are lengthy but engaging.  Each class is a wealth of insight in probability.  Pay attention and there is almost no need to read the book to understand the topics and do the homework.  The clarity with which he delivers the material is inspiring.  Homework usually requires careful thinking, but never impossible.

Excellent, engaging and brilliant professor. Makes statistics interesting through use of important historical events and stories. Book due out soon, "The Goddess of Chance", Oxford University Press. Outstanding Prof!
Don't take too many notes.  Just get the formulae from the book and listen to Santosh's lectures.  He is brilliant.
Extremely good at lecturing on tough material.  Brings a real excitement to a tough and generally dry field.  Hands out a very significant amount of work though.  Be prepared to bust your butt.
excellent, excellent teacher!  Very clear and well prepared class material.  I'm in grad school now, and I make frequent reference to the notes I took while in Santosh's class.
Not a good professor.  Stay away from his class.

Prof. Vijay Kumar is very experienced in teaching. Even difficult topics in Robotics can be understood with relatively less effort if taught by him. He is also very rigorous in theory. He has written his own notes/manuscripts for the derivation of many equations and theories, which are very good references for students.
Really enjoyed working on my independent studies with him. Excellent experience.

Worst prof. Countless assignments each week, at the end of the semester, you'll finish 21++ assignment. He loves using mathematics prove concept, but the proving progress is extremely not serious.  I mean he let two obvious different variables as the same, ridiculous. He won't curve, and grade tough. Assigs have logic issues but due won't be delay.

reviews.comment: One of the best profs ever!
Awesome prof

If there is such a title as a cyber samurai this prof is it. He is a far cry from all the other profs who just preach. This prof does a lot of deep show and tell. He is also a real internetwork expert and cyber hacker. He recommends key books but honestly he should be teaching the guys who write the books. He is an expert++ and demands precision.

Hardest class I have taken. You need to know everything - servers, routers, switches, vmware, programming, bits, bytes. Prof knows all this inside and out - has real world practical experience. You will get no credit for something that sort of works - class demands very high level of precision and perfection to become a cyber kung fu master.

For professor the network either works or not - very binary 1 or 0. No partial credit after so much work. Always available to help understand concepts but you need to put in lots lots  of effort and show you want to learn. All classes hands on and  shows you so many tricks. Part of DoD cyber warrior program - pretty serious stuff.
clear on assignments and easy on grading.  easy work load for the week.

Professor is way passionate about security and really knows it inside out. His eyes and red lips get way intense when he is so focused on topic. Prof is always online 2am, 5am, 10pm - doesn't matter he can do a conference call anytime. He needs perfection so you need make sure you learn the material and core concepts.

Very very hard to get an A in this class. Instructor is always helpful and I always get response in less that 5 minutes. Instructor is a real cyber and internet expert - he teaches but also does detail show in class. No credit for attendance like other classes but if you want to learn you need to show up or you lose a lot.

The professor is quite open to discussion and  the class shows how to use the security tricks. He is not big on deadlines but be careful - if you don't do the work in time you will be lost on the next week's homework. Make sure to submit all papers before last day of class and you are good. Don't expect to get any credit for showing up to class.

Several points:
1. His voice is too low, handwriting is unreadable.
2. The course is unreasonably fast. It is impossible to do most of the proofs carefully with this speed. Though big picture is important, even people previously saw the topic before couldn't follow up is too much.
3. Too many typos and even dumb mistakes in the homework and exams.
